source: Near the park I make tea and then I take a break.
Just before eight I fold the laundry without rushing.
In the living room I take the trash out and then I take a break.
Right now I turn off the lights before the kitchen gets busy.
In the hallway I rinse a glass while the room is quiet.
While waiting I water the plants without rushing.
On the train I find my keys before it gets late.
On the bus I air out the room and keep things simple.
In the afternoon I close the curtains without rushing.
Near the park I close the curtains while my tea steeps.
In the library I lock the door while the room is quiet.
After the rain I wash the dishes with no stress.
At the desk I tidy my desk with a steady pace.
After the rain I take a short walk while the light is still good.
In the afternoon I call my sister with a clear head.
Later I put the book back so I can focus later.
At the office I check the mailbox before it gets late.
Around eight I start the dishwasher so I can focus later.
At the counter I reply to an email with no stress.
Before the call I make tea so I can focus later.
On the bus I wipe the counter before it gets late.
After class I put the book back with a steady pace.
